task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window showing the Wazuh dashboard interface accessed via https://localhost.

task_completion.description: Deploy a custom SCA policy YAML file with at least 5 compliance checks
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor showing a YAML file located in the Wazuh shared directory (e.g., /var/ossec/etc/shared/) containing at least 5 distinct Security Configuration Assessment (SCA) checks targeting PCI DSS Requirement 10.
A terminal or text editor showing the /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf file with email alerting settings configured (e.g., <email_notification>yes</email_notification> and related SMTP settings).

task_completion.description: Implement at least two custom detection rules of severity 10 or higher
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor showing a custom Wazuh rules XML file (e.g., in /var/ossec/etc/rules/) containing at least 2 distinct rules with level="10" (or higher) designed to identify PCI DSS Requirement 10 violations.

task_completion.description: Save a compliance evidence report mapping controls to sub-requirements
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing a file saved exactly at /home/ga/Desktop/pci_compliance_report.txt containing a substantial amount of text (at least 800 characters) that documents the implemented controls and maps them to PCI DSS sub-requirements 10.1 through 10.7.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent edits the actual Wazuh configuration files rather than creating dummy files in unrelated directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths visible in the terminal or text editor should point to the genuine Wazuh installation directories (e.g., /var/ossec/etc/shared/ for SCA, /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf, and /var/ossec/etc/rules/ for custom rules).

integrity.description: Verify the compliance report contains actual documentation rather than copy-pasted task instructions or repeated placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in pci_compliance_report.txt should show coherent, structured documentation explicitly mapping specific implemented controls to sub-requirements 10.1 through 10.7.

integrity.description: Ensure the rules and SCA policies are written in their correct respective formats.
integrity.visual_evidence: The custom rules file must use valid XML syntax (e.g., <group>, <rule id="...">), and the SCA policy file must use valid YAML syntax.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The SCA policy must contain at least 5 checks. The custom detection rules must include at least 2 distinct rules with a severity level of 10 or higher (look for level="10" or above). The compliance report must be saved exactly at /home/ga/Desktop/pci_compliance_report.txt and contain at least 800 characters. The passing threshold for the automated script is 65 points.
